bullets: Implemented state-of-the-art variant-aware Genomic Foundation Models.
Optimized existing GFM pipelines for faster data loading and throughput.
Developed multiple demos and presentations for company product pitches.
Currently working on seamless user AI interactions in a local sandbox setting.
Implemented mutation-aware Antimicrobial Resistance Software achieving 91% accuracy and 0.93 AUC by pretraining on Mistral and ChemBERTA-2.
Trained multi-billion parameter LLMs across multiple nodes and GPUs, surpassing SOTA benchmarks using Mixture of Experts and Transformer architectures with FSDP.
Created the largest genomics data corpus with over 1 trillion tokens using parallelized data generation and efficient dataloaders.
Evaluated 10+ large-scale foundation models across 25+ SOTA benchmarks.
Created 5+ novel genomic tokenizers leveraging variants and annotation, improving long-range task performance by over 9%.
Worked on End-to-End Design De-Rendering — a first-of-its-kind problem exploring de-rendering in the domain of design images.
Built unified pipeline to layer, segment, and inpaint design components using attention visualizations from transformer models.
Processed a corpus of over 160,000 images for detailed layering and de-rendering.
Filed a US Patent based on this work.

company_sub: Center for Computational Natural Sciences and Bioinformatics
bullets: Advised by Prof. Chittaranjan Hens for M.S. Thesis on decomposition of large-scale matrices for first-passage problems in stochastic evolution.
Used UNets to emulate long-term dynamics in stochastic systems achieving 92% classification accuracy.
Worked with Prof. Deva Priyakumar on active learning and Bayesian optimization for de novo drug discovery pipelines — achieving over 75% benchmark time reduction.

company: IRIC — Institute for Research in Immunology and Cancer
bullets: Modeled protein-target interactions for AML computationally using AutoDock.
Removed natural ligands and proposed novel Deep Learning-based docking techniques.
Explored various ligand-protein docking configurations under Dr. Brian Wilhelm.

company_sub: Systems Engineering of the Natural and the Artificial
bullets: Advised by Prof. Raghunathan Rengaswamy.
Implemented a paper that computationally processes thermal sensor images to estimate room occupancy.
Achieved 90% accuracy in high-activity areas and 100% in normal-activity zones.
Developed and tested features under the Ruby on Rails team.
Created 10+ feature-based pull requests, reviewed 1000+ lines of code, and migrated 100+ DB attributes.
Fixed async job bugs and created paper-trail versioning for record-keeping.
